name: Baklava
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds chopped nuts (I use 1 lb walnuts and 1/2 lb almonds)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the chopped nuts and spices in a bowl.
2. In a 9x13 pan, brush some melted butter on the bottom.
3. Place a piece of phyllo dough over the butter, then brush with more butter.
4. Add another layer of phyllo, repeating until you have 6 sheets in total.
5. Sprinkle one-third of the nut mixture over the layers.
6. Build another layer of buttered phyllo using 4 sheets and sprinkle with another third of nuts.
7. Repeat with another 4-sheet layer of buttered phyllo and sprinkle with the remaining nuts.
8. Add a top layer of buttered phyllo, using about 8 sheets. If there is any melted butter left, pour it over the baklava in the pan.
9. Using a sharp knife, cut the baklava into diamond shapes, stopping at the bottom layer of phyllo. You should get 24 to 36 diamonds, depending on size.
10. Bake in a preheated oven set to 300°F for 1 hour.
11. Cool for about an hour before pouring hot syrup over cool baklava (a friend recommended this).
12. The baklava will soak up the syrup as it cools. It is better if made ahead of time and can be kept unrefrigerated for several days.
SYRUP:
1. In a saucepan, mix sugar, water, honey, and lemon.
2. Bring to a boil and simmer for 15 minutes, skimming off foam.
3. Press the lemon against the side of the pan to release some juice into the syrup while it cooks.
4. Remove the lemon and pour hot syrup over cool baklava.
